title: Interior lighting system
abstract: An interior lighting system has arrangement of lighting panels mounted around a window for providing lighting around the outside of the window. The light provided depends on the exterior lighting which passes through the window. In this way, contrast between the window and the wall around the window can be reduced.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. An interior lighting system comprising: an arrangement of lighting panels (16) mounted at least on opposite lateral sides of a window and adjacent to the window for providing lighting visible as luminance at a wall surface around the outside of the window; a light sensor arrangement (72) for sensing the exterior lighting level outside or through the window; and a controller (70) for controlling the lighting delivered by the arrangement of lighting panels (16) in dependence on the sensed exterior lighting level. 2. A system as claimed in claim 1, wherein each lighting panel (16) comprises a reflector (20) for reflecting part of the exterior light which passes through the window to the wall surface. 3. A system as claimed in claim 2, wherein each lighting panel comprises a light source (22) for directing light to the reflector. 4. A system as claimed in claim 1, wherein each lighting panel (16) comprises a backlight (30) and a diffusive cover (32). 5. A system as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein each lighting panel comprises a first portion for overlapping an edge of the window thereby for receiving part of the exterior light which passes through the window and a second portion for overlapping a wall around the edge of the window. 6. A system as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein each lighting panel (16) is controllable to provide a non-uniform light output over its area, wherein the light output is greater at an edge for mounting adjacent or overlapping the window than at an edge for mounting away from the window. 7. A system as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein each lighting panel (16) further comprises sound absorbing material. 8. A system as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the light sensor arrangement (72) comprises a light sensor (74) facing the window and a light sensor (76) facing the wall surface adjacent the window. 9. A system as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the color temperature and/or color point of the lighting provided by the lighting panel is controllable. 10. A system as claimed in claim 9, wherein the light sensor arrangement comprises a light sensor facing the window which has color sensing capability. 11. A system as claimed in claim 10, wherein the color temperature and/or color point of the lighting provided by the lighting panel is controllable in dependence on the sensed color. 12. A system as claimed in any preceding claim comprising: first and second lighting panels (16) for mounting at opposite lateral sides of the window; or first to fourth lighting panels (16) for mounting all around the sides of the window. 13. A method of providing interior lighting, comprising: sensing exterior lighting outside or through a window between an interior space and the exterior; providing lighting from a wall surface around the outside of the window using an arrangement of lighting panels (16) mounted at least on opposite lateral sides of the window and adjacent to the window, wherein the method comprises controlling the lighting delivered by the arrangement of lighting panels (16) in dependence on the sensed exterior lighting. 14. A method as claimed in claim 13, comprising: overlapping a first portion of each lighting panel with an edge of the window 

 and overlapping a second portion of each lighting panel with a wall around the edge of the window; and reflecting part of the exterior light which reaches the first portion to the wall surface; or passing part of the exterior light which reaches the first portion through a diffusive panel cover. 15. A method as claimed in claim 13 or 14, comprising: sensing the color of the light entering the interior space through the window, and controlling the color temperature and/or color point of the lighting provided by the lighting panel in dependence on the sensed color.
